q: A changing magnetic field could produce a current.
In a DC, the electrons flow steadily in the same direction all the time.
The current delivered by a battery is a direct current.
The current delivered by a generator is an alternating current.
The current that flows in a flashlight is a direct current.
The current that flows in a cell phone is an alternating current.
The current that flows in a laptop is a direct current.
The current flowing in power lines is direct current.
The normal household electricity from a wall outlet is direct current.
The voltage used in Myanmar is between 220 V and 240 V.
A galvanometer is used to measure very small current.
The alternating current induced in a rotating coil is constant with time.
An AC generator is a device for generating direct current.
The alternating current is universally used for household electricity.
Magnetic flux is a vector quantity.
The SI unit of magnetic flux is the weber (Wb).
One weber is equal to one tesla-metre squared.
If the magnetic field is perpendicular to the surface area, the magnetic flux is zero.
If the magnetic field is parallel to the surface area, the magnetic flux is zero.
The induced emf is proportional to the rate of change of magnetic flux.
The direction of the induced current is given by Lenz's law.
Lenz's law is based on the law of conservation of energy.
The frequency of alternating current used in Myanmar is 50 Hz.
Self-induction is sometimes called the inertia of electricity.
An electric motor converts mechanical energy into electrical energy.
A generator converts mechanical energy into electrical energy.
The back emf opposes the applied voltage in an electric motor.
The back emf in a motor is zero when the motor is at rest.
Mutual induction is the induction of an emf in one coil by the changing current in another coil.
Transformers can change the voltage of a direct current.
The core of a transformer is built up of thin iron sheets called lamination.
The lamination of the core increases the effect of eddy currents.
A step-up transformer increases the voltage and decreases the current.
A step-down transformer increases the voltage.
In an ideal transformer, the output power is equal to the input power.
Soft iron is used for the core of a transformer because it is easily magnetized.
Eddy currents cause energy loss in the form of heat in the transformer core.
The secondary wavelets have the same speed as the primary wavelets.
The secondary wavelets have the same frequency as the primary wavelets.
The transformer coil connected to an alternating voltage source is called the secondary coil.
The transformer coil connected to an electrical device is called the secondary coil.
AC generators are also called alternators.
In an electric motor, electrical energy is used to rotate the armature.
The ignition system of motor cars uses an induction coil.
In the ignition system, sparking occurs at the sparking plug.
The alternating current changes its direction periodically.
The emf is induced only when there is a change in magnetic flux.
The transformer ratio is given by Vs / Vp = Ns / Np.
In a step-up transformer, Ns is greater than Np.
Mechanical energy is used to rotate the armature in a generator.
